transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 59 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 68 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 71 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 72 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1357 of file websocket/transport.c.

1358                                                                                        {
1359   if (!name) {
1360     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1361     return NULL;
1362   }
1363 
1364   if (!url) {
1365     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1366     return NULL;
1367   }
1368 
1369   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1370   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1371   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1372   if (!protocol_end) {
1373     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1374     return NULL;
1375   }
1376 
1377   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1378   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1379 
1380   // Find port (if specified)
1381   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1382   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1383 
1384   char host[256] = {0};
1385   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1386   char path[256] = "/";
1387 
1388   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1389     // Port is specified
1390     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1391     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1392       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1393       return NULL;
1394     }
1395     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1396     host[host_len] = '\0';
1397 
1398     // Extract port
1399     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1400     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1401       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1402       return NULL;
1403     }
1404   } else {
1405     // No port specified, use default
1406     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1407     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1408       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1409       return NULL;
1410     }
1411     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1412     host[host_len] = '\0';
1413   }
1414 
1415   // Extract path
1416   if (path_start) {
1417     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1418     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1419   }
1420 
1421   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1422 
1423   // Allocate transport structure
1424   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1425   if (!transport) {
1426     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1427     return NULL;
1428   }
1429 
1430   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1431   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1432       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1433   if (!ws_data) {
1434     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1435     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1436     return NULL;
1437   }
1438 
1439   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1440   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1441 
1442   // Create receive queue
1443   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1444   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1445     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1446     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1447     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1448     return NULL;
1449   }
1450 
1451   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1452   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1453   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1454     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1455     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1456     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1457     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1458     return NULL;
1459   }
1460 
1461   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1462   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1463   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1464   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1465 
1466   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1467     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1468     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1469     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1470     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1471     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1472     return NULL;
1473   }
1474 
1475   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1476     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1477     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1478     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1479     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1480     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1481     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1482     return NULL;
1483   }
1484 
1485   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1486     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1487     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1488     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1489     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1490     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1491     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1492     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1493     return NULL;
1494   }
1495 
1496   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1497     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1498     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1499     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1500     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1501     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1502     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1503     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1504     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1505     return NULL;
1506   }
1507 
1508   // Allocate initial send buffer
1509   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1510   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1511   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1512     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1513     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1514     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1515     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1516     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1517     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1518     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1519     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1520     return NULL;
1521   }
1522 
1523   // Create libwebsockets context
1524   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1525   // NOTE: Define ONLY "acip" protocol for WebSocket connections
1526   // libwebsockets provides built-in "h1" and "http" protocols for WebSocket upgrade handshake.
1527   // When local_protocol_name="h1" and protocol="acip" are specified:
1528   // 1. libwebsockets uses built-in h1 (HTTP/1.1) for initial connection
1529   // 2. HTTP upgrade happens automatically (101 Switching Protocols)
1530   // 3. Connection switches to "acip" protocol using our custom callback
1531   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1532       {
1533           "acip", // ACIP protocol - used after WebSocket upgrade handshake completes
1534           websocket_callback,
1535           0,      // Per-session data - using connect_info.userdata instead
1536           524288, // RX buffer size
1537           0,      // ID
1538           NULL,   // User pointer (set from connect_info.userdata)
1539           524288  // TX packet size
1540       },
1541       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1542   };
1543 
1544   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1545   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1546   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1547   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1548   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1549   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1550   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1551 
1552   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1553   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1554   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1555   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1556   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1557   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1558   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1559   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1560   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1561   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1562 
1563   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1564   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1565   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1566   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1567 
1568   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1569   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1570   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1571       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1572       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1573   };
1574   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1575 
1576   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1577   lws_log_init_client();
1578 
1579   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1580 
1581   if (!ws_data->context) {
1582     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1583     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1584     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1585     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1586     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1587     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1588     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1589     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1590     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1591     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1592     return NULL;
1593   }
1594 
1595   // Initialize connection state flags BEFORE calling lws_client_connect_via_info
1596   // The callback might fire immediately, so all fields must be ready
1597   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1598   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1599   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1600 
1601   // Connect to WebSocket server
1602   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1603   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1604   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1605   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1606   connect_info.address = host;
1607   connect_info.port = port;
1608   connect_info.path = path;
1609   connect_info.host = host;
1610   connect_info.origin = host;
1611   // Use h1 (HTTP/1.1) protocol for WebSocket upgrade with acip as the subprotocol
1612   // - local_protocol_name="h1": libwebsockets' built-in HTTP/1.1 handler for WebSocket upgrade
1613   // - protocol="acip": Request this subprotocol from the remote server (Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header)
1614   // libwebsockets internally uses h1 regardless of the protocol name, so we use h1 directly
1615   connect_info.local_protocol_name = "h1"; // libwebsockets built-in HTTP/1.1
1616   connect_info.protocol = "acip";          // Remote subprotocol to request from server
1617   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1618   connect_info.ssl_connection =
1619       use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1620   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1621 
1622   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1623   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1624   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1625   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1626     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1627     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1628     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1629     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1630     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1631     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1632     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1633     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1634     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1635     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1636     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1637     return NULL;
1638   }
1639 
1640   // Initialize transport
1641   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1642   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1643   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1644 
1645   // Start service thread immediately - it will drive TLS handshake with frequent polling
1646   // The service thread checks is_destroying and context validity before calling lws_service,
1647   // so it's safe to start it right away without delays.
1648   ws_data->service_running = true;
1649   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1650     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1651     ws_data->service_running = false;
1652     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1653     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1654     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1655     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1656     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1657     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1658     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1659     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1660     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1661     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1662     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1663     return NULL;
1664   }
1665   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1666 
1667   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1668   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1669   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1670   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1671   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1672   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1673 
1674   // Register transport
1675   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1676 
1677   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1678   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1679 
1680   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1681   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1682   return transport;
1683 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), cond_destroy(), cond_init(),
    websocket_transport_data_t::connection_failed,
    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_NETWORK, acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_destroying, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, lws_log_init_client(),
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT, NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_running,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_thread, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t *
  crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming
  clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1695 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1696                                                                                        {
1697   if (!name) {
1698     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1699     return NULL;
1700   }
1701 
1702   if (!wsi) {
1703     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1704     return NULL;
1705   }
1706 
1707   // Allocate transport structure
1708   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1709   if (!transport) {
1710     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1711     return NULL;
1712   }
1713 
1714   // Allocate transport-specific data
1715   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1716       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1717   if (!ws_data) {
1718     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1719     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1720     return NULL;
1721   }
1722 
1723   // Initialize receive queue
1724   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1725   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1726     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1727     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1728     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1729     return NULL;
1730   }
1731 
1732   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1733   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1734   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1735     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1736     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1737     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1738     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1739     return NULL;
1740   }
1741 
1742   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1743   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1744   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1745   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1746   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1747     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1748     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1749     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1750     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1751     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1752     return NULL;
1753   }
1754 
1755   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1756   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1757   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1758   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1759   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1760   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1761 
1762   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1763     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1764     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1765     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1766     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1767     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1768     return NULL;
1769   }
1770 
1771   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1772     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1773     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1774     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1775     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1776     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1777     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1778     return NULL;
1779   }
1780 
1781   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1782     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1783     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1784     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1785     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1786     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1787     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1788     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1789     return NULL;
1790   }
1791 
1792   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1793     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1794     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1795     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1796     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1797     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1798     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1799     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1800     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1801     return NULL;
1802   }
1803 
1804   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1805     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1806     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1807     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1808     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1809     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1810     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1811     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1812     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1813     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1814     return NULL;
1815   }
1816 
1817   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1818     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1819     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1820     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1821     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1822     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1823     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1824     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1825     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1826     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1827     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1828     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1829     return NULL;
1830   }
1831 
1832   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1833   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1834   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1835   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1836     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1837     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1838     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1839     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1840     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1841     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1842     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1843     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1844     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1845     return NULL;
1846   }
1847   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1848 
1849   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1850   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1851   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1852   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1853   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1854   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1855            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1856   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1857 
1858   // Initialize transport
1859   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1860   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1861   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1862 
1863   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1864 
1865   // Register transport
1866   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1867 
1868   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1869   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1870 
1871   return transport;
1872 }






References cond_destroy(), cond_init(),
    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT,
    NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.4
    ascii-chat
